commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the TUI pairing-required disconnect hint to name `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`, adds an adjacent test assertion, and fixes the skills Python pre-commit dependency specifier.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +1, Other 0. Total +1 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main returns the old pairing hint and the disconnect handler posts that hint into the TUI chat log. I did not run a live pairing flow in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(tui): include pairing approval command in recovery hint (Fixes #67618)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #67618.

When the TUI disconnects with a pairing-required error, it currently tells the user to run `openclaw devices list` and then "approve your request ID" without ever naming the actual approval command.

This keeps the scope narrow:
- update the pairing recovery hint to include `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`
- keep the status line and pairing-detection behavior unchanged
- add a focused regression assertion on the help text

## Tests and validation

```bash
node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/tui/tui.test.ts
git diff --check
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
- pairing-required TUI disconnect guidance should tell the operator the exact approval command to run after `openclaw devices list`

Environment tested:
- local source checkout on this branch

Evidence after the fix:
- `resolveGatewayDisconnectState()` now returns:
  - `Pairing required. Run \`openclaw devices list\`, then \`openclaw devices approve <requestId>\`, then reconnect.`
- `src/tui/tui.test.ts` now asserts the hint includes `openclaw devices approve <requestId>`

Observed result:
- the recovery text now names the exact CLI step instead of making the operator infer it from the pending request list

What was not tested:
- a live TUI pairing flow against a running gateway

Proof limitations:
- the focused Vitest invocation currently fails in this checkout before tests run because of a pre-existing `getOAuthProviders is not a function` startup error outside this TUI change

## Risk

Low. This is a copy-only TUI hint change with a matching unit-test assertion. No pairing protocol, gateway behavior, or CLI flow changed.
